title: Offered to drop her home, 30-year-old married woman gang-raped in Haridwar hotel
description: Three individuals have been apprehended in connection with the alleged gang-rape of a woman in Haridwar. The incident occurred when a man offered the victim a ride home, leading her to a hotel where a friend joined him. The hotel manager was arrested for alleged negligence in verifying guest identities. Police are investigating further with CCTV footage.

title: Two important US government departments are ending use of Anthropic products after Trump’s order
description: Following President Trump's directive, the US Treasury Department and the Federal Housing Finance Agency have ceased using Anthropic's AI technology. This action stems from a dispute over Anthropic's refusal to permit its AI for mass surveillance or autonomous weapons. The Pentagon also flagged the company as a national security risk, impacting defense contractors.

title: Tech employees across America send open letter to Pentagon on Anthropic
description: Hundreds of tech employees have signed an open letter urging the Department of War and Congress to withdraw Anthropic's "supply chain risk" designation. This action followed a failed contract negotiation, leading to a presidential directive to cease using Anthropic's technology. The letter argues this precedent punishes American innovation and urges a resolution through commercial channels.

title: Pentagon head to military officers: You cannot study in these 22 universities, see list
description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has removed 22 universities, including Harvard and MIT, from the Pentagon's list for military officer education, citing "anti-American" sentiments. New partnerships are proposed with institutions like Liberty University and George Mason, focusing on AI and space, despite previous collaborations with removed schools on critical research.
